text:
There was an old man named Arthur. He lived alone in a house surrounded by ivy. Arthur loved everything about bees.

One day the bees flew away. Heartbroken, Arthur looked everywhere for them.

After days wandering aimlessly, he came across an old owl. The owl told him about a secret colony. Arthur followed the owl to their nest underground.

There were thousands of bees. Arthur cried tears of joy.

The old owl took flight again. "Go back home."

Arthur never forgot those bees.

Years turned rusty old shoes into an antique shop. He sold one item at a time. No new bees flew through. But every birthday, he planted young trees.

One bird took notice.

It flew down from an oak tree.

The bird sat on Arthur’s head.

A gentle breeze made it dance.

It chirped.

It sang.

Just another bee.

A gentle family grew together. Ravens flew past with wings filled with interest.

Every year they sang.

Every year Arthur planted more trees
